informal_statement.text: Given a slice of closed-open intervals [start, end), return the maximum number of mutually non-overlapping intervals selectable from the input.
preconditions: for every input interval, start < end
required_properties: the returned count equals the size of some subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals from the input
no subset of pairwise non-overlapping intervals from the input is strictly larger than the returned count
intervals sharing only an endpoint (interval i ends where interval j starts) are considered non-overlapping
edge_cases: all intervals mutually disjoint (result equals len)
all intervals overlap a common point (result is 1)
intervals touching at a single endpoint

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: every interval satisfies start < stop.
Feasibility: the returned count is witnessed by some subset of the input that is pairwise non-overlapping.
common_failure_modes: confusing index selections with value selections

semantic_units.description: Optimality: no pairwise non-overlapping subset of the input is strictly larger than the returned count.
common_failure_modes: stating only feasibility (admits the trivial return 0)

semantic_units.description: Endpoint convention: two intervals that share only an endpoint are non-overlapping.
common_failure_modes: using strict inequality instead of non-strict at the boundary

semantic_units.description: Empty input: the result is 0 when the input is empty.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ivs : Intervals), (∀ i ∈ ivs, i.start < i.stop) → ∃ (sel : List Nat), Feasible ivs sel ∧ sel.length = intervalScheduling ivs
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every input interval has strictly positive length, there exists a feasible selection whose size equals the returned count.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (ivs : Intervals) (sel : List Nat), (∀ i ∈ ivs, i.start < i.stop) → Feasible ivs sel → sel.length ≤ intervalScheduling ivs
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: When every input interval has strictly positive length, no feasible selection is strictly larger than the returned count.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a b : Interval), a.stop = b.start → NonOverlap a b
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: Intervals touching at a single endpoint are non-overlapping.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit precondition is asserted by the implementation; it accepts any list of (start,end) pairs.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: def nonoverlap (iv1 iv2 : Int × Int) : Prop := iv1.1 < iv2.2 ∧ iv2.1 < iv1.2 → False
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Intervals are treated as half-open [start,end); the code uses the condition s >= last_end to decide non-overlap, which corresponds to allowing sharing a single endpoint.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: let sorted := intervals.toList.qsort (fun a b => a.2 ≤ b.2); ∀ i j, i < j → (sorted.get i).2 ≤ (sorted.get j).2
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The algorithm sorts intervals by nondecreasing end time (the Rust sort_by_key(|iv| iv.1) uses the end coordinate as key).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
/-- Invariant: after processing the first k intervals in sorted order, `count` equals the size of a set of chosen non-overlapping intervals among those k, and `last_end` is the end of the last chosen interval (or none if count=0). -/ 
∀ (k : Nat),
  let prefix := (sorted.take k);
  let (count,last_end) := (run_prefix prefix); 
  (count = length (chosen prefix)) ∧
  (count = 0 ↔ last_end = none) ∧
  (last_end ≠ none → ∀ iv ∈ chosen prefix, iv.2 ≤ last_end.get)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Maintain that the greedy selection built so far is a maximum-cardinality non-overlapping subset for the processed prefix, and track the end time of the last selected interval.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (iv : Int × Int),
  (last_end ≠ none → iv.1 ≥ last_end.get) →
  (chosen' = chosen ∪ {iv} → chosen' is nonoverlapping)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: When the algorithm accepts an interval (s >= last_end), adding it preserves mutual non-overlap for the chosen set.
If the next interval is compatible with the current greedy schedule, then the optimal maximum cardinality for the extended prefix increases by 1, matching the greedy increment.

If the next interval overlaps the current greedy last_end, then no optimal solution for the extended prefix can increase the maximum cardinality by including this interval, so the greedy does not need to count it.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: interval_scheduling intervals = count_final ∧ count_final = opt_count intervals
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After iterating through all intervals, the returned `count` equals the maximum cardinality of a set of mutually non-overlapping intervals.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (intervals : List (Int × Int)),
  interval_scheduling intervals = Nat.card {S | S ⊆ intervals ∧ pairwise_nonoverlapping S ∧ ∀ T ⊆ intervals, pairwise_nonoverlapping T → |T| ≤ |S| }
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The function computes the maximum size of a pairwise non-overlapping subset of the given intervals (maximum-cardinality interval scheduling).
